title: French municipal elections: Socialists and LFI set aside differences to strike local deals
subhead: After denouncing LFI leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon's 'intolerable antisemitic remarks,' the Socialists allied with the radical left in key cities ahead of the runoff votes on Sunday.

title: Senior US counterterrorism official resigns to protest Iran war
subhead: In his resignation letter to Donald Trump, Joseph Kent said 'Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.'

title: Trump agitated and exasperated by continued blocking of strategic Strait of Hormuz
subhead: The US president criticized his European allies for their lack of enthusiasm after proposing an operation aimed at restoring freedom of navigation for oil tankers blocked in the Persian Gulf.

title: Michelin Guide 2026: Michaël Arnoult, the only new three-star chef
subhead: The chef at Les Morainières earned a coveted third star in the latest edition of the Michelin Guide, which was unveiled in Monaco on Monday. It was a triumph for the reserved master chef.

title: Jacques Audiard and Merry Alpern: The copyright dispute that ended well
subhead: The French filmmaker Audiard acknowledged that he had drawn on American photographer Alpern's images in 1997 to create a music video for the song 'La nuit je mens' by Alain Bashung.

title: Ravens use memory, not predators, to find kills
subhead: A study conducted in Yellowstone National Park in the United States finds that corvids return to the sites of previous feasts thanks to sophisticated strategies that had completely eluded researchers until now.

title: How Epstein made himself indispensable to MoMA's former chair Leon Black
subhead: Disclosed files reveal close ties between the late financier and the billionaire art market funder. Emails, testimonies and financial documents raise new questions about the businessman's involvement in Jeffrey Epstein's sex trafficking networks.
